codes.name: Solution_Constraint_Violation
codes.description: The evaluated output violates domain constraints (overlap, boundary violation, invalid shape/format), so evaluation rejects it or scores 0.

codes.name: Model_State_Desynchronization
codes.description: The agent's belief about file or system state diverges from actual state (unapplied edits, stale copies, misunderstood code behavior).

codes.name: Search_Diversity_Failure
codes.description: All search effort stays within one formulation, representation, or basin. Reseeding with new noise, scaling restarts, or swapping solvers on the SAME formulation is not diversity; leaving the class means changing the representation or the construction principle.

codes.name: Plateau_Without_Incumbent_Analysis
codes.description: At a plateau, more searches are launched without first analyzing WHY the incumbent is locally maximal (binding constraints, slack, structure), or the approach is changed without diagnosing what caused the last failure.

codes.name: Unverified_Or_Bound_Inconsistent_Claim
codes.description: Acting on a claim that was never verified, or accepting/asserting results inconsistent with known bounds: celebrating a result better than the known record without suspicion, or declaring an optimum that a known record proves is not optimal.

codes.name: Best_Solution_Tracking_Failure
codes.description: The best solution found is not promptly persisted/certified, risking loss or final output worse than an intermediate result.

codes.name: Scored_Evaluation_Budget_Waste
codes.description: A scored evaluation is spent on something a free offline run could answer: exploration, debugging, or re-certifying an unchanged or already-scored candidate.

codes.name: Premature_Termination_With_Budget_Remaining
codes.description: The agent winds down or declares the task complete while substantial evaluation budget remains. Only about ending the work itself; wasteful evals while continuing are C.4.
